title: Valid Anagram
description:
Two strings are anagrams of each other when one can be rearranged, letter for letter, into the other -- same characters, same counts, just shuffled into a different order. Write a function that takes strings `s` and `t` and reports whether `t` is a rearrangement of `s`.

**Example**

```
valid_anagram("rat", "tar")
# -> True   ("tar" is "rat" with its letters shuffled)

valid_anagram("rat", "car")
# -> False  (different letters entirely)
```
hints: Different lengths can never be anagrams.
Count letter frequencies in each string and compare the counts.

solutions.name: Character counts
solutions.explanation: Anagrams must have the same length and the same multiset of characters. Tally the letters of each string with a counter and compare the two tallies directly.
solutions.complexity: Time O(n), Space O(k) where k is the alphabet size
